title: Senior/Staff Designer Portfolio Analysis
summary: Analysis of 8 portfolios from designers at Google, Apple, Meta, Airbnb, OpenAI, Stripe, Uber, and Amazon. Identifies patterns that signal seniority and separate strong portfolios from weak ones.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Simon Pan
strengths: 70% process / 30% outcome split in body, but metrics front-loaded
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: The homepage is a metrics dashboard. Every card shows quantified impact before the click.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Sebastien Gabriel
strengths: Separate writing section signals thought leadership
Product scale (billions of users) replaces explicit metrics
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: At staff level, the product name IS the metric. Writing signals strategic influence.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Angela Li
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: Confidence in substance over portfolio aesthetics is itself a seniority signal.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Karolis Kosas
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: Letting 'previously Stripe, now OpenAI' establish credibility instantly.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Pratibha Joshi
strengths: Design system project alongside product work
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: Showing design system work alongside product work signals systems thinking.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Elise Fu
strengths: Impact/Metrics section BEFORE background and process
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: Results-first structure captures attention in the first 10 seconds.

portfolios_analyzed.name: Tom Petty
strengths: Design principles as a named section
portfolios_analyzed.takeaway: Articulating design principles per project signals strategic thinking.

senior_signals: Lead with impact, not process (Minto Pyramid)
Problem reframing is the hero moment, not the final UI
Business context opens the case study, not user frustration alone
Constraints and tradeoffs are featured, not hidden
2-5 case studies maximum, quality over quantity
Case study structure varies per project type
Writing or talks section signals organizational influence
junior_signals: Follows the same template for every case study
Explains everything in detail rather than curating
Says 'we' for everything without clarifying ownership
No evidence of failed approaches or pivots
nda_strategies: Focus on process artifacts (screeners, affinity maps, architecture)
Password-protected deep dives shared during interviews
Explicitly state the NDA constraint (builds trust)

implications_for_rob: SUS score (low 70s to high 80s) is the strongest real metric. Put it in the APM headline.
Aysa numbers (15k views, $2.1M seed round) are strong. Keep them prominent.
Don't force metrics on Warehouse, Keytrn, or Sinta. Use specific descriptive outcomes instead.
The tab vs. single-page story in APM is a senior signal (problem reframing). Make it more prominent.
Keytrn implementation (built the scraper, design system) is a major differentiator. Keep it visible.
The portfolio site itself (built in Astro/React) is a signal. Make it visible somewhere.
Before/after comparisons on Honeywell work would be high impact when assets are available.
